children.text: the causes that render livestock nonambulatory;
the humane treatment of nonambulatory livestock; and
the extent to which nonambulatory livestock may present handling and disposition problems for stockyards, market agencies, and dealers.

children.heading: Authority
children.label: b Authority
children.text: Based on the findings of the report, if the Secretary determines it necessary, the Secretary shall promulgate regulations to provide for the humane treatment, handling, and disposition of nonambulatory livestock by stockyards, market agencies, and dealers.

children.heading: Administration and enforcement
children.label: c Administration and enforcement
children.text: For the purpose of administering and enforcing any regulations promulgated under subsection (b), the authorities provided under sections 10414 [] and 10415 [] shall apply to the regulations in a similar manner as those sections apply to the Animal Health Protection Act [ et seq.]. Any person that violates regulations promulgated under subsection (b) shall be subject to penalties provided in section 10414.7 U.S.C. 83137 U.S.C. 83147 U.S.C. 8301
